steps.title: Set the y-values equal
steps.content:
At an intersection, both equations have the same $y$-value. Since one equation is $y=7$, substitute $7$ into $y=x^2-2$:
$$
7=x^2-2
$$

steps.title: Solve for x
steps.content:
Add $2$ and take square roots:
$$
x^2=9
$$
$$
x=\pm 3
$$

steps.title: Write the coordinates
steps.content: Both points have $y=7$, so the intersections are $(3,7)$ and $(-3,7)$. The answer is A.
